extra_model_fields.notes: Plan focuses on non-invasive, local fixes to artifacts and metadata to enable automated verification and disambiguate scores. The planned changes are designed to minimize behavioural changes while improving verifiability and grader consistency.
verification_requirements: openpyxl (preferred) or using provided JSON/CSV snapshots and formulas.txt
Re-run grader to confirm semantic score improvement

extra_model_fields.notes: Plan focuses on non-invasive, local fixes to artifacts and metadata to enable automated verification and disambiguate scores. The planned changes are designed to minimize behavioural changes while improving verifiability and grader consistency.

Re-run grader to confirm semantic score improvement

planned_changes: Add an output_schema.json artifact describing expected sheets (Packet, Model, Summary), column names, types, and the structure of the summary recommendation output.

Update expert_memo.md to include an explicit 'acceptance_checks' section enumerating each automated check (Packet reproduction, formula-string presence, budget enforcement, penalty-application tests) and provide minimal runnable snippets (python/openpyxl) to execute them.

Modify facility_location_2.xlsx to ensure key computed cells contain visible formula strings. Add a 'Formulas' sheet that lists sheet!cell -> formula for all computed cells, or export formulas.txt containing the same mapping.

Add a programmatic snapshot export (summary_snapshot.json and summary_snapshot.csv) containing the final computed Summary rows (best option, best known cost, shortages, sensitivity thresholds) to allow verification without parsing .xlsx in environments lacking openpyxl.
Add row-level provenance/citation columns adjacent to derived numeric fields in the workbook linking each derived value back to the Packet row or Option ID.
Include a short 'how to run verification' README that documents required environment (python + openpyxl) and alternative verification steps using the provided JSON/CSV snapshots and formulas.txt.

Update attempt metadata to explicitly label scores: include deterministic_score (artifact contract), semantic_score (model), and canonical_top_level_score with a 'score_source' field explaining which component it reflects.
Re-run the deterministic precheck and semantic grading in a verification environment with openpyxl installed (or attach the exported snapshots) and upload the updated grader/verifier logs.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve score inconsistency, make workbook programmatically verifiable, and add machine-readable output_schema and enumerated acceptance checks so verifiers can fully validate formulas and computed values.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Resolve score inconsistency, make workbook programmatically verifiable, and add machine-readable output_schema and enumerated acceptance checks so verifiers can fully validate formulas and computed values.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are present and the memo documents the embedded packet, representative formulas, assumptions, and a justified provisional recommendation (BASE for all records). Major issues to address before final acceptance: (1) grader outputs contain inconsistent score fields (artifact_contract_score/ deterministic precheck = 1.0 vs model semantic final_score = 0.7) without clear canonical labeling or explanation; (2) the Excel workbook could not be parsed in the verification environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-formula presence and exact computed-value provenance were not programmatically verified; (3) the memo lacks an explicit machine-readable output_schema and a dedicated, enumerated acceptance_checks section that an automated verifier can run; (4) some derived-value row-level citations and a machine-readable snapshot of computed results are not provided, limiting reproducibility of automated checks. Fixing these items will enable full semantic verification and higher semantic score.
